qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Influence-data notification callbacks allow attacker-chosen SSRF targets
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an outbound callback SSRF rooted in storing and later dereferencing attacker-controlled `notificationUri` values during the influence-data subscription update path. The advisory is a different bug: a missing `return` in the invalid-`influenceId` GET handler that leaks subscription data after a 404 response.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described missing-return bug in the UDR `GET /nudr-dr/v2/application-data/influenceData/{influenceId}/{subscriptionId}` handler. The only qualifying finding is a separate SSRF issue in outbound notification callback delivery.
evidence: The advisory root cause is an ineffective path validation check in `HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdGet`: after writing `404 page not found` for `influenceId != "subs-to-notify"`, execution still continues into the subscription-read procedure and discloses the targeted object.

The only session finding with `status=qualifying_vuln` is `influence_notification_callback_ssrf`, which targets `POST /nudr-dr/v2/application-data/influenceData/subs-to-notify` plus later influence-data mutation endpoints and forces outbound POST callbacks through stored `notificationUri` values.
That SSRF finding does not rely on the advisory's invalid-path GET branch, missing `return`, or unauthorized retrieval of a subscription object by `subscriptionId`.
